METADATA.Keywords: MeOH, SNG, Methanol to SNG, Methanol reforming, Synthetic natural gas

INTERNAL CONNECTORS.DESCRIPTION: Intermediate syngas from MeOH reforming unit into the methanator

MeOHReform.Unit Description: This unit models the autothermal reforming (ATR) of methanol combined with water gas shift (WGS) reactors and a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit to produce a syngas with stoichiometric ratio [(H2−CO2)/(CO+CO2)] = 3 suitable for SNG production via methanation. The unit consumes methanol (12 103 kg/h) and produces syngas (12 709 kg/h) . Total electrical demand is 219 kW.
MeOHReform.Connectors Description: Methanol, water and oxygen are fed into the ATR reformer at 1 bar and compressed/pumped to 35 bar internally. The unit outputs syngas to the methanator and separated CO2 at low pressure. Electricity is consumed for O2 compression and feed pumping. No steam connectors are required as the ATR is autothermal. Net water flow accounts for condensate recovery.

Connectors.DESCRIPTION: Consumed methanol feedstock into the MeOH reforming unit
Consumed water feedstock into the MeOH reforming unit

MeOHReform.Heat Streams Description: Oxygen is intercooled between the four compression stages and then preheated before entering the ATR. The methanol/water mixture is preheated in three stages (liquid sensible, vaporisation, superheating). The syngas leaving the ATR is cooled in two stages after the WGS reactors to condense water before the PSA.

Heat Streams.NAME: qt_h110

The unit consumes methanol (12 103 kg/h) and produces syngas (12 709 kg/h) . Total electrical demand is 219 kW._feed

MeOHReform.Equipments Description: The main equipment items cover the four-stage O2 compressor train with intercoolers, the feed pumps (methanol and water), the heat exchanger network (preheating and cooling), and the ATR reformer vessel.
MeOHReform.Operating Cost Description: The fixed and variable operating costs includes manpower, fixed cost of operation, taxes etc.

SyngasMethanator.Unit Description: The SyngasMethanator converts syngas from the MeOH reforming unit into substitute natural gas (SNG) via catalytic methanation at 35 bar. The process uses three adiabatic fixed-bed reactors with intercooling to manage the strongly exothermic Sabatier reaction. The unit produces 4 081 kg/h of CH4.
SyngasMethanator.Connectors Description: Syngas is consumed to produce methane. The reaction produces water and CO2.

Connectors.DESCRIPTION: Consumed water feedstock into the MeOH reforming unit
Intermediate syngas from MeOH reforming unit into the methanator

The process uses three adiabatic fixed-bed reactors with intercooling to manage the strongly exothermic Sabatier reaction. The unit produces 4 081 kg/h of CH4.unconverted

Connectors.DESCRIPTION: CO2 separated in PSA unit and unconverted CO2 from methanation section

SyngasMethanator.Heat Streams Description: Syngas is preheated and goes throught three fixed-bed reactor followed by intercooling.
SyngasMethanator.Equipments Description: Investment is captured globally via Spec_MethntrCost (300 EUR/kW_CH4) in the VARIABLES sheet. The equipment entries below serve as cross-check references.
SyngasMethanator.Operating Cost Description: The fixed and variable operating costs includes manpower, fixed cost of operation, taxes etc.
